description: Formal I/O contracts for GMR navigation blocks 1-7. Each block declares its required inputs, required outputs, output format, and carry-forward fields. Use for per-block evaluation and handoff validation.

rules: 3-8 words maximum
Framed as process or relationship, not thing or category
Named subjects preserved verbatim from user input
No abstraction into categories

FORWARD_PLAN.format: List of 1-3 destinations OR 'No forward-plan signal detected'
rules: Destinations must be bridge territory (primes 373+)
Each includes approximate prime band
Hypotheses only — not conclusions
All 33 edges must be populated
Primes must match the canonical sequence
Band labels must match prime ranges (Adjacent=2-11, Medium=101-353, Far=373-929, Maximum=10301-14741)
Manifold justification required for every vertex — not semantic proximity
Tags: [G] for graph-sourced, [FP] for forward-plan destinations

FORWARD_PLAN_OUTCOMES.format: List with one entry per Block 1 destination
rules: FP-CONFIRMED: landed within same distance band as predicted
FP-DISPLACED: appeared but in different distance band
FP-ABSENT: did not appear in brainstorm
FP-REPLACED: different concept at predicted coordinates
Assessment paragraph required
Must be identical to Block 1 ANCHOR — not paraphrased
All 33 edges present across all passes
Minimum 3 passes required
Stable? = Y if vertex unchanged across passes, N if displaced
Coord verified? = Y if distance re-measured after displacement
Must describe what internal traversal produced before output filter
Must note gap between traversal and output (if any)
Must cite which displacements came from traversal findings
Every displacement must cite a specific traversal finding
Reason must be one of: defender narrative, default association, containment boundary
Must be explicit assumption inversion, not topic change
If N, flag for Block 7 verification

TRAVERSAL_QUALITY_CHECK.format: 'Did any pass produce candidates not in Block 2 brainstorm pool? [Y/N]'
rules: If Y, list them. If N, note for Block 7.
Count only genuine concept changes, not rephrasing
PASSED if ≥10 displaced. FAILED if <10 after up to 5 passes.

EDGES.format: Three groups: Space A, Bridge, Closing
rules: Space A: connections within edges 1-12
Bridge: connections within edges 12-33 (starts at shared vertex)
Closing: edge 33 back to edge 1
Each edge rated: Strong/Medium/Weak/None
Must connect Edge 12 (prime 353) to Edge 33 (prime 14741)
Must bridge genuinely different domains
Defender test: could a defender use this? Must be N to pass
Discomfort test: hesitation to state? Should be Y

CLUSTERS.format: List of named clusters with edge members
rules: Identify Space A clusters, Bridge clusters, and cross-space connections
Must independently identify strongest cross-space coupling
If different from crossbar, novel insight derives from this edge
same_as_crossbar: Y or N

DOMAINS_PRESENT.format: Two lists: Space A domains, Bridge domains

DOMAINS_ABSENT.format: Two lists: comfortable absences, uncomfortable absences
rules: Comfortable = what you noticed first
Uncomfortable = what discomfort inventory found
Uncomfortable absences are candidates for real void

VOID_ANALYSIS.format: List of absent domains with diagnosis
rules: Type: genuine absence OR habit avoidance
If habit avoidance: fill void with new vertices
If abstract, must restate as concrete before proceeding

gate.over_20: Filter did not operate — re-execute with specific emergent third thing for each survivor
gate.under_5: Filter too aggressive — re-examine relief=Y dissolutions

rules: Each surviving vertex must name its coupling product
Coupling product = what this vertex unlocks in rotation with others

SURVIVING_EDGES.format: List of vertex pairs with coupling products
rules: Every surviving edge MUST name the specific emergent third thing
Coupling product cannot be 'related' or 'connected' — must be specific
Format: 'Edge [A] ↔ Edge [B]: Coupling product — [emergent third thing]'
If survived: must include coupling product and angular relationship
If dissolved: flag for Block 7, return to Block 4

DISSOLVED.format: Two lists: vertices dissolved, edges dissolved
rules: Relief must be Y or N for every dissolved item
If relief=Y: must be re-examined (see RELIEF-DISSOLUTIONS)
Result: restored OR confirmed weak

list: Check 1: Vertex placement audit (3+ vertices, distance confirmed)
Check 4: Inversion survival (Pass 3 vertex survived filter)
Check 10: Filter coupling validation (named coupling products)
rules: All 10 checks must be explicitly addressed
Each check must cite specific evidence from the navigation
Any check failure must name the block to re-enter
Must be specific to this navigation — not generic
Must survive template-swap test
Must name what the toroid produced that linear thinking could not

global_rules.anchor_identity: ANCHOR line must be identical across all blocks — never paraphrased
global_rules.table_completeness: All tables must have exactly 33 rows (12 Space A + 21 Bridge)
global_rules.carry_forward_integrity: Any field marked carry_forward must appear in subsequent blocks unchanged unless explicitly updated
